metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2927
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.2: Inhibited LTD but normal LTP in Numb-cKO mice"

Fig.3: "Synaptic mGlu1 is reduced in Numb-cKO mice"

Fig.5: "mGlu1-LTD and mGlu1-STP are normal, but mGlu1 trafficking is inhibited in Numb-cKO mice"
- "It has been shown that mGlu1 is internalized (63-66) and recycled back into the cell membrane (65) upon ligand exposure. Because trafficking may affect receptor targeting at the cell membrane, we investigated whether Numb is involved in the internalization and recycling of mGlu1. DHPG (100 μM) was applied to acute cerebellar slices for 10 min, and the level of synaptic mGlu1 was measured 30 min (for endocytosis) or 180 min (for recycling) after DHPG application. In control slices, synaptic mGlu1 decreased to 34 ± 4% of the basal level at t = 40 min (n = 4; P < 0.01) but returned to 77 ± 8% at t = 190 min (n = 4; P > 0.05) (Fig. 5 E and F). The down- and up-regulation of mGlu1 expression in response to such agonist challenges was similar to results reported in previous work in other wild-type mice (66-68). In contrast, synaptic mGlu1 was not changed either 30 min or 180 min after DHPG application to Numb-cKO slices (t = 40 min: 96 ± 8%; t = 190 min: 103 ± 8%; P > 0.05 compared with baseline) (Fig. 5 E and F), suggesting that Numb is required for the internalization and recycling of mGlu1 in PCs.". Experimental description: - "Mice were kept under temperature-controlled conditions on a 12-h/12-h ..."
